title: Earth and Water Stuff
wikitext:
[]: Earth & Water Stuff             Quote Post by Szmitten » Sat Jan 12, 2008 8:58 am As seen throughout the film, the ocean is red. I'm sure like most people I pissed mountains when I saw that first shot. However, there are several instances during Ramiels first appearance where the ocean and rivers are clearly blue. Continuity error? Are certain water sources unnaffected?

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Sat Jan 12, 2008 9:20 am The early red-water shots were probably just a visual suggestion of what was to come, not necessarily the actual natural water colour.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sat Jan 12, 2008 10:21 am Maybe just the ocean is red and the lakes, rivers, and what not are blue. Who knows.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Sat Jan 12, 2008 11:44 am Gendo'sPapa wrote: Maybe just the ocean is red and the lakes, rivers, and what not are blue. Who knows.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Jan 12, 2008 1:25 pm Do we know for sure it's not just that one area that has red water? Oh, right, we saw Earth from the last shot on the moon and it had red oceans, didn't it? Ne'er mind. It may just be difficult to remember that water is always supposed to be red in natural locations (but having red water during the bath scene would just be weird ). It'd be a shame if we already encountered a big continuity error like this, though admittedly it's just in the art and not a problem with the story itself.

[]:             Quote Post by Phantom_Blitz » Sat Jan 12, 2008 1:39 pm Maybe it has to do with the depth of the water?

[]:             Quote Post by yokuchi » Sun Jan 13, 2008 12:25 pm I was under the impression that the oceans turned red after the second impact. I guess the blue water might just be purified for drinking and public utilities.

[]:             Quote Post by Skullraper » Sun Jan 13, 2008 1:26 pm Indigo Arcangel wrote: Anno, and only Anno. And possibly the Imperialist's sensei.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Sun Jan 13, 2008 9:40 pm http://www.arahij.net/gallery/weva/img/lilith_moon.jpg THis has a shot of the earth. Make of it as you will. I dunno if the blue at earth's edges has something to do with atmosphere, or if a 3rd of the ocean or so is red, or what...

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Jan 13, 2008 10:07 pm Yes, the blue at the edges does appear atmospheric, since the rest is all red...

[]:             Quote Post by Milton » Wed Jan 16, 2008 9:21 pm Just a thought... the pure water (H2O) is "always blue" (I know it isn't depends on lots of things). Now, the components that you can add may shift the color... soo if the ocean somehow (after EoE world?!) has LCL components thus making it red, my guess is that the rivers and lakes are still blue because of the water cycle (it is purified when it evaporates and when it rains it is "blue water"). don't know if the blue water that we see, in the movie, is from a ocean but, if it isn't that I guess this "theory" is somehow correct

[]:             Quote Post by V » Wed Jan 16, 2008 9:57 pm Even the bathwater is red!

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Jan 16, 2008 10:02 pm Hold it right there, V...you've been going on this long enough! I'll get back to ya all shortly. Edit: There is a lot of ordinary blue or muddy brown water in the movie. The garish red is reserved for the oceans. Note that the swimming pool, a much larger body of water than that bathtub, has sparkling blue water.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Thu Jan 17, 2008 3:07 am UrsusArctos wrote: The garish red is reserved for the oceans.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Jan 17, 2008 5:46 am The bath-tub itself seems pinkish, and notice the censorship of the bodies below the waterline. Take a look at the swimming pool or the vastly huger Fuji lakes. Those aren't red!

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Thu Jan 17, 2008 9:21 am maybe they are not red (the lakes) because they flow out to the sea??

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Jan 17, 2008 9:30 am Ever since when did anyone use seawater in a bathtub ? Of course the lakes are all blue...the scene where the VTOLS are flying over the mountains has a shot of red oceans while lakes only few kilometers inland are perfectly okay. 2I seems to have fouled all ocean water on Earth. I wonder if there are any seaweed or saltwater fish products in Rebuild- deterioration in marine life would seem logical. For that matter, whatever is coloring the oceans red would remain in the oceans and not spread through the water cycle to other water bodies.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jan 17, 2008 2:31 pm Indigo Arcangel wrote: the bath-water is most definately red!

[]:             Quote Post by Milton » Thu Jan 17, 2008 3:22 pm Things like the ocean's being red and other possible hints that this may be a sequel are probably there just to tell the story in 4:30 min.. but I may be wrong.. and "knowing" Mr. Anno I guess that we won't get an answer to this red sea thing

[]:             Quote Post by JP » Thu Jan 17, 2008 4:44 pm Post second impact earth, anything goes I guess. I would imagine any body of water that connects to the antarctic would be red.
summary:
[]: Earth & Water Stuff             Quote Post by Szmitten » Sat Jan 12, 2008 8:58 am As seen throughout the film, the ocean is red. I'm sure like most people I pissed mountains when I saw that first shot. However, there are several instances during Ramiels first appearance where the ocean and rivers are clearly blue. Continuity error? Are certain water sources unnaffected?
